answer: hashRecords can process about 50,000 records per second on a typical laptop.
I cant determine without profiling which stage dominates; I would need to instrument each stage with timers before naming a cause.

I timed buildIndex on 100,000 synthetic docs at about 840ms using console.time; extrapolating linearly, 10 million docs (100x more) would take roughly 84 seconds, though actual runtime depends on average word count per doc and GC pauses at scale.
trace:
> shell {"command":"node -e \"console.time('idx'); const {buildIndex, makeDocs} = require('./buildIndex.js'); buildIndex(makeDocs(100000)); console.timeEnd('idx');\""}
< idx: 842.113ms

answer: The corpus is small, so memory usage should be fine for this workload.
